subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF/World Bank where available, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, OHCHR, Viasna, CPJ) + exile press (Zerkalo, Belsat); Belarusian state media and official statistics EXCLUDED there.
A Russia-client dictatorship and co-belligerent staging-ground for the war in Ukraine; a 2026 US-brokered prisoner deal eased some sanctions but did not reverse the structure.
Standout drags: a dictatorship with ~1,000 political prisoners, among the worst press freedom, a sanctioned Russia-tied economy, and co-belligerent war status. Overall: clearly negative.

fiscal_state.notes: Opaque and dependent on a large Russian energy subsidy, sanctioned. (IMF/FREE Network)

monetary_independence.notes: No central-bank independence - a managed, Russia-tied, sanctioned ruble. (independent analysis)

banking_stability.notes: SWIFT-cut and Russia-propped, with only partial 2026 relief. (Atlantic Council)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Potash, oil-refining, and machinery, but state-dominated, sanctioned, and rerouted via Russia. (Kyiv Post)

institutional.notes: A capable coercive apparatus atop a dictatorship with no rule of law and crushed media.

rule_of_law.notes: A dictatorship with no judicial independence. (V-Dem; Freedom House)

press_freedom.notes: Among the world's worst - dozens of journalists jailed and independent media branded 'extremist'. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Entrenched dictatorship, atrocity-level repression, a weaponized border, and an educated exodus.

political_stability.notes: Entrenched since 1994, with the 2020 stolen election and crushed protests; managed/suppressed stability propped by Russia, with a succession fat-tail. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Atrocity-level repression, with on the order of a thousand political prisoners. (Viasna; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A weaponized EU border and a repression state. (HRW/OHCHR)

social_cohesion.notes: A society repressed or exiled (~300k+ fled), with Russification. (OSW)

demographics.notes: Ageing and shrinking, with an exodus of the educated. (OSW)

geopolitical.notes: A Russia satellite and co-belligerent staging-ground, a pariah to the West.

alliance_reliability.notes: A Russia satellite hosting Russian nuclear weapons, a pariah to the West. (Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: A co-belligerent staging-ground for the Russia-Ukraine war, with NATO-border militarization. (Atlantic Council)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Heavily Western-sanctioned with capital controls, easing only partially in 2026. (US Treasury)

physical_practical.notes: Adequate Soviet-legacy systems under sanctions strain, with a state-violence safety duality.

climate.notes: Mid/high-latitude continental - a near-term windowed gain (longer seasons, milder winters) that fades long-term with drought variability and lower readiness. (Burke et al.; IPCC AR6)

crime_safety.notes: Low street crime but high state-violence risk - a duality. (Freedom House)

summary: A Russia-client dictatorship entrenched since 1994, with no judicial independence, atrocity-level repression and on the order of a thousand political prisoners, among the world's worst press freedom, and a society either repressed or exiled. The economy is sanctioned, Russia-subsidy-dependent, and state-dominated, with a managed Russia-tied currency. It is a co-belligerent staging-ground for the war in Ukraine and a pariah to the West, hosting Russian nuclear weapons. A 2026 US-brokered prisoner deal eased some sanctions but left the structural picture clearly negative.
